cards.title: Biopsychosocial Model of Pain
cards._legacyHtml:
Engel's Biopsychosocial Model: Pain = Biological + Psychological + Social factors.
Biological: Tissue damage, nociception, inflammation, central sensitisation, genetics.
Psychological: Catastrophising (strongest predictor of chronic pain), fear-avoidance, depression, anxiety, self-efficacy.
Social: Work status, litigation, cultural pain expression, family reinforcement, social isolation.
Implication: Treating biology alone fails in chronic pain — all 3 dimensions must be addressed.
Yellow Flags (psychological risk for chronicity): Belief that pain = damage; fear of movement (kinesiophobia); passive coping; work dissatisfaction; low self-efficacy.

cards.title: Pain Neuroscience Education (PNE)
cards._legacyHtml:
Definition: Therapeutic education reconceptualising pain as an output of the brain (danger signal) rather than a direct measure of tissue damage.
Key concepts to teach patients:
  • Pain does not equal tissue damage
  • Central sensitisation: nervous system becomes hypersensitive (alarm system on low threshold)
  • Brain can modulate pain via descending inhibitory pathways (endorphins, GABA)
  • Movement is safe — hurt ≠ harm
Evidence: PNE alone ↓ pain intensity, ↓ catastrophising, ↑ function in LBP, fibromyalgia, CRPS.
Delivery: 2–3 sessions, 45 min each; use metaphors (alarm system, nervous system as volume knob).

cards.title: Central Sensitisation — Clinical Identification
cards._legacyHtml:
Central Sensitisation (CS): Amplification of neural signalling in CNS → pain hypersensitivity beyond site of injury.
Clinical Features:
  • Allodynia — pain from normally non-painful stimuli (touch, light pressure)
  • Hyperalgesia — exaggerated pain from mildly painful stimuli
  • Widespread pain beyond original injury site
  • Pain disproportionate to tissue findings
  • Poor response to analgesics/NSAIDs
  • Sleep disturbance, fatigue, cognitive fog
CS Inventory (CSI): Validated questionnaire; score ≥40 = likely CS.
Conditions with CS: Fibromyalgia, IBS, TMD, tension headache, interstitial cystitis, CRPS.

cards.title: Supplements for Pain Management
cards._legacyHtml:
Magnesium Glycinate: 300–400 mg/night. NMDA receptor antagonist → ↓ central sensitisation; muscle relaxant. Evidence: fibromyalgia, migraine prevention, neuropathic pain.
Omega-3 (EPA + DHA): 2–3 g/day with food. ↓ prostaglandin E2, ↓ IL-1β, ↓ TNF-α. Evidence: RA, LBP, neuropathic pain, migraine.
Curcumin (with piperine): 500–1000 mg/day (BCM-95 or liposomal form). NF-κB inhibitor, COX-2 inhibitor. Evidence: OA, RA, IBD, neuropathic pain.
Palmitoylethanolamide (PEA): 600 mg BD. Endocannabinoid system modulator. Evidence: neuropathic pain, fibromyalgia, sciatica. No psychoactive effects.

cards.title: LBP (Low Back Pain) — Integrative Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Phase 1 — Acute (0–2 weeks): Cryotherapy 20 min 4×/day. TENS conventional 80–100 Hz. Positional rest (not bed rest). PNE: reassure movement is safe.
Phase 2 — Subacute (2–6 weeks): Hot packs pre-exercise. IFT 80–100 Hz. Lumbar traction (if disc component). MET for hip flexors/quadratus lumborum. Core stabilisation programme (McGill Big 3: bird-dog, curl-up, side plank).
Phase 3 — Chronic (>6 weeks): PNE full course. Yoga (Pavanamuktasana, Bhujangasana, Shavasana). MFR thoracolumbar fascia. Magnesium + Omega-3. Graduated walking programme.

cards.title: Fibromyalgia — BNYS Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Diagnostic Criteria: Widespread pain ≥3 months + 11/18 tender points OR 2016 ACR criteria (WPI ≥7 + SSS ≥5).
BNYS Management:
  • Exercise: Aquatic therapy / hydrotherapy — 30 min, 3×/week. Water reduces weight-bearing, warm water ↓ spasm.
  • PNE: Full 3-session course — high evidence in FM.
  • Sleep: CBT-I protocol; Yoga Nidra 30 min before bed.
  • Supplements: Magnesium 400 mg/night + D3 2000 IU + CoQ10 200 mg.
  • Balneotherapy: Neutral bath 37°C, 20 min daily — parasympathetic activation.
  • Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR): 8-week programme.

cards.title: Neuropathic Pain — Integrative Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Causes: DPN, PHN, CRPS, trigeminal neuralgia, chemotherapy-induced PN, HIV PN.
Assessment: DN4 questionnaire (score ≥4 = neuropathic); painDETECT; Monofilament test.
BNYS Protocol:
  • TENS acupuncture-like: 2–4 Hz, motor intensity, 30 min daily along dermatome.
  • LLLT: 830 nm, 4–6 J/cm² along nerve distribution. 10 sessions.
  • Alpha-lipoic acid: 600 mg/day IV or oral — strongest evidence for DPN.
  • Magnesium + B12 (methylcobalamin 1500 mcg/day): Nerve repair.
  • Yoga: Balance and proprioception training; Pawanmuktasana series.
  • Warm foot baths: Tepid 38°C, 20 min daily for DPN.

cards.title: PNE Scripts — Patient Communication
cards._legacyHtml:
Pain ≠ Damage script: 'Pain is your brain's best guess that you are in danger — it is not a meter that directly measures tissue damage. Phantom limb pain proves this: pain in a limb that no longer exists.'
Central sensitisation metaphor: 'Your nervous system is like a smoke alarm that has become oversensitive — it goes off when you burn toast. The problem is in the alarm sensitivity, not the fire. We need to turn the sensitivity back down.'
Movement is safe: 'Hurt does not equal harm. When you have chronic pain, movement may hurt but that does not mean it is damaging you — your nervous system has learned to be protective. We gradually retrain it.'
